text:
There once was a wise old tortoise. He lived in a quiet meadow amongst beautiful flowers. One sunflower stood out especially.

One day, a squirrel scampered towards the big flower. "Oh," it chattered animatedly. "You look bright orange!"

The squirrel shook harder. "That must be special!"

The wise tortoise waved his slow branch curiously. "Why are you so cheerful?"

"I love bright things!" exclaimed the squirrel.

After some thought, the tortoise smiled. "I know. Maybe you'd think I do."

He gathered some petals. When the squirrel tasted them, it chirped praises. That night, stars watched as twinkling lights blinked like twinkling leaves.

So, our wise tortoise learned friendship pays large.

One autumn evening. "You want a pear?"

He popped it into his beard. The squirrel nodded. They nibbled and talked.

"The fields are big."

The fox eyed hungry sheep nearby. "You wouldn comer me."
